title: James Hoffmann's Ultimate V60 Technique
author: James Hoffmann

steps.description: Pour 2-3x coffee weight. Swirl the V60 gently to saturate all grounds.
Pour in slow circles to 60% of total water (170g).
Pour remaining water to reach 250g total.
Give the V60 a gentle swirl to knock grounds off the walls.
Wait for complete drawdown. Target 3:00-3:30 total.

notes: Hoffmann's technique emphasizes a longer bloom with a swirl and a continuous main pour. Boiling water recommended for light roasts.

title: Tetsu Kasuya 4:6 Method
author: Tetsu Kasuya
attribution: Credit: Tetsu Kasuya — 2016 World Brewers Cup Champion

steps.description: First 40% pour: 60g. Controls sweetness vs acidity.
Wait for water to drain through.
Second 40% pour: 60g (total 120g).
Wait for water to drain through.
First 60% pour: 60g (total 180g). Controls strength.
Wait for water to drain through.
Second 60% pour: 60g (total 240g).
Wait for water to drain through.
Final pour: 60g (total 300g).
Let it drain. Total time ~3:30.

notes: The first 40% (2 pours) controls sweetness/acidity balance. The last 60% (3 pours) controls strength. Uses a coarser grind than typical V60.

title: Scott Rao V60 Technique
author: Scott Rao

steps.description: Pour 3x coffee weight. Stir gently with a spoon to ensure even saturation.
Single continuous pour in slow, steady circles to 360g total.
Rao spin — gentle swirl to create a flat bed.
Wait for complete drawdown. Target 3:00-3:15 total.

notes: Rao emphasizes a single continuous main pour and his signature swirl (Rao spin) for an even extraction bed.

title: James Hoffmann's French Press Technique
author: James Hoffmann

steps.description: Add all water. Do NOT stir.
Wait 4 minutes. Let the crust form.
Break the crust with a spoon. Scoop off foam and floating grounds.
Wait 5-8 more minutes. Do NOT press the plunger down.
Gently pour without pressing. Plunger sits on top as a filter only.

notes: Hoffmann's key insight: do NOT press the plunger down. Use a medium grind, not coarse. The long total steep (9+ min) and gentle pour produce a cleaner cup than traditional French press.

title: James Hoffmann's AeroPress Technique
author: James Hoffmann

steps.description: Standard orientation (not inverted). Single paper filter, rinsed.
Add all water quickly.
Stir briefly to ensure all grounds are wet.
Insert plunger to seal. Wait 2 minutes total.
Gently swirl the AeroPress.
Press gently for 30 seconds. Stop when you hear hissing.

notes: Hoffmann recommends standard orientation, boiling water, and a finer grind than most AeroPress recipes. Simple and repeatable.

title: Alan Adler's Original AeroPress Recipe
author: Alan Adler

steps.description: Standard orientation. Paper filter in cap.
Pour water at 175°F / 80°C.
Stir for 10 seconds.
Press gently for 20-30 seconds.

notes: The inventor's original recipe uses much lower temperature (175°F) than most specialty coffee recipes. Quick and forgiving.

title: Chemex Classic Brew Guide
author: Chemex

steps.description: Saturate grounds evenly. Wait for bloom to settle.
Pour in slow circles, keeping water level consistent (300g total).
Continue pouring in circles (500g total).
Final pour to 700g total.
Wait for complete drawdown. Target 4:00 total.

notes: Chemex's thick paper filter produces a very clean cup. Use a coarser grind than V60 to compensate for slower flow.

title: Kalita Wave Standard Technique
author: Kalita

steps.description: Wet all grounds evenly. The flat bottom ensures even saturation.
Pour in concentric circles to center. 140g total.
Let water draw down slightly.
Pour to 230g total.
Let water draw down slightly.
Final pour to 320g total.
Wait for complete drawdown. Target ~3:30 total.

notes: The Wave's flat bottom and wave filter promote even extraction with less technique sensitivity than a V60.

title: Clever Dripper Standard Recipe
author: Community Standard

steps.description: Pour all water over grounds while Clever sits on counter (valve closed).
Gentle stir to ensure all grounds are saturated.
Cover and steep for 3 minutes.
Place Clever on mug or server. Valve opens, coffee drains through filter.

notes: The Clever Dripper combines immersion and filtered drawdown. Very forgiving and consistent. Great for beginners.

title: Fellow Stagg Pour-Over Guide
author: Fellow

steps.description: Pour to saturate all grounds. The ratio aid helps measure.
Slow pour in circles to 150g total.
Brief pause.
Pour to 235g total.
Brief pause.
Final pour to 320g total.
Wait for complete drawdown. Target ~3:30 total.

notes: Fellow's Stagg X/XF is a double-walled pour-over with a flat-bottom basket. Slightly faster flow than Kalita.

title: Aiden Light Roast Default Profile
author: Fellow

steps.description: Machine blooms with 2.5x coffee weight at 96°C.
3 single-serve pulses at 30s intervals, 96°C each.

notes: Fellow's default light roast profile. Higher temp and more pulses to fully extract light roasts.

title: Aiden Dark Roast Default Profile
author: Fellow

steps.description: Machine blooms with 2.0x coffee weight at 90°C.
Single pulse brew at 90°C.

notes: Lower temp and fewer pulses to avoid over-extracting dark roasts. Produces a smoother, less bitter cup.

title: Moccamaster Best Practices
author: Technivorm
attribution: Credit: Technivorm — adapted from official guide

steps.description: Use #4 paper filter. Pre-wet filter with hot water. Discard rinse water.
Fill reservoir. Add grounds to basket.
At halfway, stir the grounds in the basket gently with a spoon.
Let machine finish. Total brew time ~4-6 min.
Swirl the carafe to mix. Serve.

notes: Moccamaster brews at a fixed ~96°C. Grind and dose are your main variables. Stirring mid-brew helps even extraction.
